title: Stichorkis arcuata
taxonomy: Stichorkis arcuata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Stichorkis is a small, primarily Asian genus within the subtribe Malaxideae. Stichorkis arcuata specifically belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Malaxideae. Further classification may involve its species-level identification within the Stichorkis genus.
uses: Stichorkis arcuata has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant's properties are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. In some cultures, its extracts are used to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems.
